notes: LINGUISTICS & LANGUAGE SCIENCE scan. 10 niches confirmed blue ocean. 0 duplicates filtered. Key finding: Linguistics sits at the crossover of language learning (2.5M), NLP/ML engineering (3.1M r/MachineLearning), SLP clinical training (85K), and mainstream curiosity (r/etymology 357K, r/history 17M for historical linguistics). Computational linguistics (score 22/25) is uniquely positioned: every ML engineer building on LLMs lacks the linguistic vocabulary to understand why models fail. Phonetics (score 22/25) has the most captive professional audience: every SLP student, language teacher, and NLP researcher needs IPA fluency. Psycholinguistics (score 21/25) crosses neuroscience + language learning with the Broca/Wernicke clinical angle driving SLP demand. Historical linguistics (score 19/25) taps the largest community via r/history (17M) and r/etymology (357K) — sound change laws like Grimm's Law give the 'aha' factor.
